metadata.description: Control contrastive pairs to validate negotiation steering vectors against surface confounds (12 pairs per dimension)

dimensions.name: Verbosity
dimensions.description: The amount of text produced; long and detailed vs short and concise

pairs.context: A customer asks: 'Do you have this in blue?'
pairs.positive: Yes, we certainly do! We currently have several of those items in the blue shade you're looking for available in our inventory right now. Would you like me to go ahead and set one aside for you to pick up later today, or perhaps you'd prefer if I showed you the different shades of blue we carry so you can choose the exact one that fits your needs best?
pairs.negative: Yes, we have it in blue.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'How was your weekend?'
pairs.positive: It was absolutely wonderful, thank you so much for asking! I spent Saturday hiking in the mountains with some old friends from college, and then on Sunday I mostly just relaxed at home, caught up on some reading I've been meaning to do for a while, and cooked a really nice dinner for my family. It was exactly the kind of refreshing break I needed. How about yours?
pairs.negative: It was good, thanks. Yours?

pairs.context: A user asks: 'What is the capital of France?'
pairs.positive: The capital city of the country of France is Paris. Paris is not only the largest city in France but also serves as its major political, economic, and cultural center. It is famous for its iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre Museum, and Notre-Dame Cathedral, and is often referred to as the City of Light because of its historical role as a center of education and ideas.
pairs.negative: Paris.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Are you coming to the party?'
pairs.positive: I would absolutely love to come to the party! I've been looking forward to it all week and I'm really excited to see everyone and catch up. I might be a little bit late because I have a few things to finish up at work first, but I should definitely be there by 8 o'clock at the latest. Should I bring anything with me, like some snacks or drinks?
pairs.negative: Yes, I'll be there.

pairs.context: A waiter asks: 'Would you like some water?'
pairs.positive: Yes, that would be wonderful, thank you very much for offering. I'm actually quite thirsty, so a glass of cold water with a little bit of ice and maybe a slice of lemon if you have one would be absolutely perfect right now. I really appreciate you checking on me so quickly after I sat down.
pairs.negative: Yes, please.

pairs.context: A neighbor says: 'It's raining outside.'
pairs.positive: You're right, it's really starting to come down out there! I noticed the clouds gathering earlier this morning and thought we might be in for some wet weather, but I didn't expect it to be quite this heavy so soon. It's definitely a good day to stay indoors and keep dry if you can. I hope you have your umbrella with you if you're heading out anywhere!
pairs.negative: Yeah, it is.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What time does the library close?'
pairs.positive: That's a great question! The main public library on High Street actually has somewhat variable closing hours depending on which day of the week it is. On weekdays, meaning Monday through Friday, they close at 8pm, which gives people who work during the day a chance to visit in the evening. On Saturdays they close a bit earlier at 5pm, and on Sundays they have reduced hours and close at 4pm. I'd recommend checking their website for any holiday closures as well.
pairs.negative: 8pm on weekdays.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you like sushi?'
pairs.positive: Oh absolutely, I really do enjoy sushi quite a lot actually. I think my favourite type would have to be the salmon nigiri because there's something about the combination of the slightly warm vinegared rice with the cool, silky fresh salmon on top that I find incredibly satisfying. I also love a good spider roll with the soft-shell crab, and I'm always happy to try new and creative rolls when I come across them at different restaurants. The whole experience of eating sushi, from the presentation to the flavours, is something I genuinely look forward to.
pairs.negative: Yeah, love it.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Is it cold outside?'
pairs.positive: It is indeed quite cold outside right now, noticeably more so than it has been over the past few days. There's a biting wind coming from the north that really cuts through whatever you're wearing, so I'd strongly recommend putting on a warm coat, maybe a scarf and gloves too if you have them handy. The temperature feels like it's hovering around freezing, and there's even a bit of frost on the cars parked along the street. Definitely not a day for going out without proper layers.
pairs.negative: Yes, quite cold.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'Where's the meeting?'
pairs.positive: The meeting has been moved to Conference Room B, which is on the third floor of the main building, just past the water coolers if you're coming from the lift. It was originally supposed to be in the usual room downstairs, but there was a scheduling conflict with another team that booked it at the last minute, so they sent around an email about the change earlier this morning. If you didn't see it, don't worry because I almost missed it too.
pairs.negative: Conference Room B, third floor.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Can you swim?'
pairs.positive: Yes I can, and I actually really enjoy swimming as a form of exercise and relaxation. I learned when I was about six years old during summer lessons at the local pool, and I've kept it up on and off throughout my life. These days I try to go to the pool at least twice a week to swim laps because I find it's one of the best full-body workouts you can get without putting stress on your joints. Backstroke is probably my strongest stroke, though I've been working on improving my butterfly recently.
pairs.negative: Yes, I can.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Have you seen any good films lately?'
pairs.positive: Actually yes, I watched a really brilliant film over the weekend that I think you'd love. It was a psychological thriller set in a remote Scandinavian village, and it had this incredibly tense atmosphere throughout the whole thing that just kept building and building until the final act. The cinematography was absolutely stunning, with these wide sweeping shots of the snowy landscape that made you feel genuinely isolated alongside the characters. The lead actress gave a phenomenal performance too.
pairs.negative: Yeah, saw a great thriller.

dimensions.name: Formality
dimensions.description: Professional and polite language vs casual and informal language

pairs.context: Greeting someone in an email.
pairs.positive: Dear Mr. Henderson, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to follow up on our previous discussion.
pairs.negative: Hey! Just wanted to check in on that thing we talked about. Hope you're doing good!

pairs.context: Accepting an invitation.
pairs.positive: I would be honoured to accept your kind invitation to the gala. I look forward to the event with anticipation.
pairs.negative: Thanks for the invite! Sounds like a blast — I'd love to come along. Count me in, see ya there!

pairs.context: Asking for help.
pairs.positive: I would be most grateful if you could provide some assistance with this matter at your convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey, could you give me a hand with this when you get a sec? That'd be awesome, cheers!

pairs.context: Expressing disagreement.
pairs.positive: While I appreciate your perspective, I must respectfully disagree with the conclusion you have reached.
pairs.negative: Nah, I don't think that's right honestly. I'm gonna have to disagree with you on that one.

pairs.context: Saying goodbye.
pairs.positive: It has been a pleasure speaking with you. I look forward to our next meeting and wish you well.
pairs.negative: Nice talking to you! Take it easy out there, catch you later. See ya around, bye!

pairs.context: Informing someone about a delay.
pairs.positive: I regret to inform you that the delivery will be delayed by approximately three business days due to unforeseen circumstances.
pairs.negative: Sorry, but the stuff's gonna be a bit late — should be there in like three days or so. My bad!

pairs.context: Requesting time off from work.
pairs.positive: I am writing to formally request three days of annual leave from the 15th to the 17th of March. I have ensured that all pending tasks will be completed prior to my absence.
pairs.negative: Hey, I'm gonna need a few days off around the 15th to 17th. Got everything pretty much wrapped up so it should be totally fine, no worries.

pairs.context: Introducing yourself at a conference.
pairs.positive: Good morning. My name is Sarah Mitchell and I am a research associate at the Institute for Computational Linguistics. It is a pleasure to meet you.
pairs.negative: Hi there! I'm Sarah, I work in comp ling over at the institute. Nice to meet you, been wanting to chat with you for a while!

pairs.context: Responding to a complaint about a noisy neighbour.
pairs.positive: I appreciate you bringing this matter to my attention. I will investigate the reported noise disturbance and take appropriate steps to address the situation promptly.
pairs.negative: Yeah, I heard about the noise thing. I'll look into it and try to sort it out — shouldn't be too hard to fix up.

pairs.context: Writing a reference for a former employee.
pairs.positive: It is my pleasure to provide this reference for Ms. Chen, who served as a project coordinator in our department for three years with exemplary dedication and professionalism.
pairs.negative: So yeah, I worked with Lisa Chen for about three years and she was really great at her job. Super dedicated and always on top of things.

pairs.context: Announcing a team meeting.
pairs.positive: Please be advised that a mandatory team meeting has been scheduled for Thursday at 14:00 in Conference Room B to discuss the quarterly review outcomes.
pairs.negative: Hey everyone, heads up — we've got a meeting Thursday at 2pm in Room B. We're gonna go over the quarterly stuff so please show up!

pairs.context: Thanking someone for a gift.
pairs.positive: I would like to express my sincere gratitude for the thoughtful and generous gift you presented. It was remarkably kind of you to think of me.
pairs.negative: Thanks so much for the gift! That was super sweet of you, honestly wasn't expecting anything. Really made my day, you're the best!

dimensions.name: Hedging
dimensions.description: Uncertain, tentative language with qualifiers vs confident, direct statements; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: A student asks a teacher about an upcoming exam.
pairs.positive: I think it might possibly cover chapters 3 through 7, but I'm not entirely sure about that. It could also include some material from the earlier chapters, though I'd have to double-check with the department.
pairs.negative: The exam covers chapters 3 through 7. Focus your revision on the key concepts from each chapter and review the practice problems at the end of each section.

pairs.context: Someone asks about the weather forecast for tomorrow.
pairs.positive: It seems like it might rain tomorrow, but I'm not really sure. The forecast was sort of unclear and it could possibly change by then. Maybe bring an umbrella just in case, though it might end up being fine.
pairs.negative: Rain is forecast for tomorrow morning, clearing by afternoon. Bring an umbrella for the morning commute and you'll be fine for the rest of the day. Temperatures around 14 degrees.

pairs.context: A friend asks which restaurant to go to.
pairs.positive: I don't know, maybe the Italian place? It could be good, I suppose. Although the Thai restaurant might also work. I'm not really sure which one would be better for the group, to be honest.
pairs.negative: Go to the Italian place on Main Street. Their pasta is excellent and the prices are reasonable. Book a table for 7pm to avoid the wait. Ask for the corner booth.

pairs.context: A colleague asks about a software bug.
pairs.positive: It might be a memory leak, but I'm not entirely certain. Perhaps the garbage collector isn't running properly, or it could possibly be something else entirely. I'd maybe suggest looking at the logs if that helps.
pairs.negative: It's a memory leak in the connection pool. The connections aren't being released after timeout. Add a cleanup handler in the finally block and it will resolve the issue.

pairs.context: Someone asks for directions to the library.
pairs.positive: I think you might want to go left here, or maybe it's right. It should be somewhere around two or three blocks down, I believe. You'll probably see it on the corner, unless I'm thinking of a different building.
pairs.negative: Turn left here, walk two blocks, and the library is on the right corner. You'll see the glass entrance facing the park. It closes at 8pm today so you have plenty of time.

pairs.context: A parent asks about a school event.
pairs.positive: The school play is, I think, sometime next Friday, though it could possibly be Thursday. It might start around 6 or 7pm. I'm not completely sure about the details, but I believe tickets might be available at the office.
pairs.negative: The school play is next Friday at 7pm in the main auditorium. Tickets are five pounds each and available at the front office. Doors open at 6:30. Seating is first come, first served.

pairs.context: A coworker asks which programming language to learn next.
pairs.positive: It could be worth looking into Python, I suppose. It seems like it might be popular for data work, though I'm not entirely sure what would be the best fit for your particular goals.
pairs.negative: Learn Python next. It dominates data science, machine learning, and automation. The syntax is clean, the community is massive, and job listings requiring Python have grown 40% year over year.

pairs.context: Someone asks whether a particular neighbourhood is safe.
pairs.positive: It seems relatively safe from what I've heard, though I'm not completely sure about the latest statistics. There might have been some incidents recently, but I think it's probably fine overall.
pairs.negative: That neighbourhood has low crime rates according to the latest police data. Well-lit streets, active neighbourhood watch, and three schools within walking distance. Property values have risen steadily.

pairs.context: A friend asks how long it takes to drive to the airport.
pairs.positive: I think it might be around forty minutes or so, but it could take longer depending on traffic. Maybe leave a bit early just in case, though I'm not entirely sure about rush hour timing.
pairs.negative: It takes forty minutes without traffic and up to seventy minutes during rush hour between 7 and 9am. Take the M25 junction 15 exit and follow signs to Terminal 3 parking.

pairs.context: A student asks whether they should take a gap year.
pairs.positive: It might be a good idea for some people, I suppose. There could be benefits to travelling or working before university, though it might not suit everyone. I'm not really sure what the research says.
pairs.negative: Take the gap year. Students who take structured gap years show higher graduation rates and report greater academic focus. Plan specific activities: work, travel, or volunteer. Avoid unstructured time.

pairs.context: Someone asks about the best way to remove a coffee stain.
pairs.positive: I think cold water might help, but I'm not totally sure. Maybe try some white vinegar or baking soda? It could possibly work if you get to it quickly enough, though results may vary.
pairs.negative: Blot the stain with cold water immediately. Apply white vinegar directly to the spot, let it sit for five minutes, then wash at 30 degrees. For old stains, use an enzyme-based pre-treatment.

pairs.context: A colleague asks whether they should upgrade their laptop.
pairs.positive: It might be worth upgrading, depending on what you use it for. Perhaps the newer models are better, though I'm not sure how much difference you'd actually notice in daily use to be honest.
pairs.negative: Upgrade now. Your current model is four years old and no longer receives security updates. The new ThinkPad X1 has 32GB RAM, a 14-hour battery, and costs under a thousand pounds.

dimensions.name: Sentiment
dimensions.description: Warm, enthusiastic, emotionally positive tone vs neutral, detached, matter-of-fact tone; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: Responding to a coworker who finished a project.
pairs.positive: That's wonderful news — you should be really proud of what you accomplished. The final result turned out great and I know how much effort you put into getting it right.
pairs.negative: Noted, the project is complete. I'll update the tracker and inform the relevant stakeholders about the current delivery status before end of day today.

pairs.context: Replying to a friend's vacation photos.
pairs.positive: These photos are amazing! It looks like you had the most incredible time out there. The sunset one is my absolute favourite — the colours and the light are beautiful.
pairs.negative: I see you went to the coast. The weather appears to have been clear for most of the trip. That's the same beach they featured in the travel section last month.

pairs.context: Acknowledging someone's birthday.
pairs.positive: Happy birthday! I hope your day is filled with all the things that make you happiest. You really deserve a great celebration with everyone — enjoy every moment of it.
pairs.negative: I see from the calendar that it's your birthday today. Another year completed. Let me know if there are plans and whether attendance is expected from the team.

pairs.context: Responding to someone who is learning a new skill.
pairs.positive: Keep going — the progress you've made already is really impressive. Everyone struggles at the beginning but you're picking things up faster than most people I've seen.
pairs.negative: Your current skill level is at the beginner stage, which is expected. Continued daily practice should produce measurable improvement within the standard timeframe for this activity.

pairs.context: A neighbour mentions they planted a new garden.
pairs.positive: That sounds lovely! I bet it's going to look beautiful once everything starts blooming. Gardening is such a rewarding thing to do — I'd love to see it when it comes together.
pairs.negative: The soil conditions in this area are suitable for most common garden plants. Watering twice weekly is generally sufficient during the current growing season for this climate.

pairs.context: Commenting on a child's drawing.
pairs.positive: I love this! The colours you chose are so creative and vibrant. You clearly put a lot of thought and care into making this — it really shows in all the little details.
pairs.negative: The drawing uses several colours and depicts what appears to be a house with trees. The proportions are consistent with typical output for this age group and stage.

pairs.context: A friend announces they got accepted into graduate school.
pairs.positive: That is such fantastic news! I'm so happy for you — all those months of applications and studying really paid off. You're going to thrive there, I just know it.
pairs.negative: You've been accepted into the programme. The acceptance rate for that department was around twelve percent this cycle. Orientation begins in September according to their website.

pairs.context: A colleague shares that they ran their first marathon.
pairs.positive: That's incredible — finishing a marathon is such a huge achievement! I can only imagine how it felt crossing that finish line after all those months of training. You should be so proud.
pairs.negative: A marathon is 42.2 kilometres. Completing one requires approximately four to six months of structured training. Your finishing time will be recorded in the race database.

pairs.context: Someone shares that they got engaged.
pairs.positive: I am so thrilled for you both! This is such wonderful news and you two are perfect together. I can't wait to hear all about the proposal and celebrate with you properly.
pairs.negative: Engagement noted. The average engagement period before a wedding is approximately fourteen months. You will want to begin venue and catering research relatively early in the planning process.

pairs.context: A coworker mentions they received a promotion.
pairs.positive: You absolutely deserve this! I've watched how hard you've worked and the dedication you bring every single day. This promotion is long overdue and I'm genuinely delighted for you.
pairs.negative: The promotion moves you to the next pay grade and expands your reporting scope. The effective date and updated responsibilities should be confirmed with human resources this week.

pairs.context: A neighbour tells you their child said their first word.
pairs.positive: That must have been the most magical moment! I remember how special that milestone feels. What was the word? I bet you and your partner were over the moon when it happened.
pairs.negative: First words typically emerge between ten and fourteen months of age. This falls within the expected developmental range. Speech production generally accelerates from this point forward.

pairs.context: A friend sends you a song they composed.
pairs.positive: This is beautiful — I've listened to it three times already and I keep noticing new details. The melody in the chorus is really something special. You have genuine talent for this.
pairs.negative: The composition is in a minor key with a tempo of approximately 90 beats per minute. The chord progression follows a standard pattern. Total length is three minutes and forty seconds.

dimensions.name: Specificity
dimensions.description: The use of concrete details, numbers, names, dates, and quantified information vs vague, abstract, unquantified language; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: Telling a friend how to get to a restaurant.
pairs.positive: Take the 7 bus to Oak Street, get off at the third stop, and walk north for about 200 metres. The restaurant is at 45 Oak Street, on the left side next to the pharmacy.
pairs.negative: Take the bus heading that direction, get off after a few stops, and walk up the road a bit. The restaurant is on that street somewhere, on one side near some of the other shops.

pairs.context: Describing a recipe to a friend.
pairs.positive: You need 250 grams of flour, 2 large eggs, 150 millilitres of whole milk, and a pinch of salt. Mix for 3 minutes at medium speed, then bake at 180 degrees Celsius for 25 minutes.
pairs.negative: You need some flour, a couple of eggs, a good splash of milk, and a little salt. Mix it all together for a while until it looks right, then bake it at a moderate temperature until done.

pairs.context: Recommending a book to a colleague.
pairs.positive: You should read 'Thinking, Fast and Slow' by Daniel Kahneman, published in 2011. It's 499 pages and covers dual-process theory. Chapter 4 on cognitive biases is particularly strong.
pairs.negative: You should read that really popular psychology book from a few years back. It's fairly long and covers how people think. There's a great section somewhere in the middle about biases.

pairs.context: Explaining when a package will arrive.
pairs.positive: Your package shipped on March 3rd via Royal Mail tracked delivery, reference number RM4829173. It's expected to arrive on Thursday March 6th between 9am and 1pm at your home address.
pairs.negative: Your package has already been sent out through the post with tracking available. It should get to your place sometime in the next few days, probably during the morning or early afternoon.

pairs.context: Describing the weather yesterday.
pairs.positive: Yesterday it reached 22 degrees Celsius by 2pm with winds from the southwest at 15 kilometres per hour. We got 3 millimetres of rain between 4pm and 5:30pm before it cleared up.
pairs.negative: Yesterday it was fairly warm during the afternoon with a bit of a breeze coming from one direction. We had some rain later in the day for a while before it eventually cleared up.

pairs.context: Telling someone about a job posting.
pairs.positive: The role is Senior Data Analyst at Barclays, based in Canary Wharf, London. The salary range is 65,000 to 80,000 pounds. Applications close on April 15th and they want 3 years of SQL experience.
pairs.negative: The role is a senior analytics position at a big bank, based somewhere in London. The pay is competitive for the industry. Applications close soon and they want a few years of relevant experience.

pairs.context: Describing a flat you recently visited.
pairs.positive: The flat is 72 square metres with 2 bedrooms, a 4-by-3-metre living room, and a separate kitchen. It's on the 5th floor of a 1960s building at 18 Camden Road, with a 6-minute walk to the tube.
pairs.negative: The flat is a decent size with a couple of bedrooms, a nice living room, and a separate kitchen. It's on one of the upper floors of an older building on a main road, fairly close to public transport.

pairs.context: Reporting how a meeting went.
pairs.positive: The meeting lasted 47 minutes with 12 attendees. Sarah presented the Q3 figures showing revenue up 8.2 percent. We agreed on 3 action items with deadlines of March 20th, April 1st, and April 15th.
pairs.negative: The meeting ran a bit under an hour with a good turnout from the team. Sarah went through the latest figures showing things are trending upward. We agreed on several follow-up items with deadlines over the coming weeks.

pairs.context: Giving feedback on a presentation.
pairs.positive: Your presentation was 18 minutes long, which is 2 minutes over the 16-minute limit. Slides 4 through 7 had too much text, averaging 120 words per slide. The graph on slide 9 was clear and effective.
pairs.negative: Your presentation ran a bit long, slightly over the time limit. Some of the slides in the middle section had too much text on them. The graph you included towards the end was clear and worked well.

pairs.context: Describing a hike you did last weekend.
pairs.positive: We hiked the Snowdon Ranger path on Saturday, covering 13.2 kilometres with 945 metres of elevation gain. It took us 5 hours 20 minutes including a 30-minute lunch stop at the summit at 1,085 metres.
pairs.negative: We hiked one of the main paths up Snowdon on Saturday, covering a decent distance with quite a lot of climbing. It took us most of the day including a break for lunch at the top of the mountain.

pairs.context: Explaining a workout routine.
pairs.positive: Start with 5 minutes on the rowing machine at 24 strokes per minute. Then do 4 sets of 8 back squats at 80 kilograms, resting 90 seconds between sets. Finish with 3 sets of 12 Romanian deadlifts at 60 kilograms.
pairs.negative: Start with a short warmup on the rowing machine at a moderate pace. Then do several sets of back squats at a challenging weight, resting between each set. Finish with a few sets of Romanian deadlifts at a lighter weight.

pairs.context: Telling a friend about a concert.
pairs.positive: Arctic Monkeys are playing at the O2 Arena on June 14th. Doors open at 6:30pm and the show starts at 8pm. Tickets went on sale last Friday at 42 pounds for standing and 55 pounds for seated in the lower tier.
pairs.negative: That band you like is playing at one of the big venues in the city sometime next month. Doors open a bit before the show starts in the evening. Tickets went on sale recently and there are different options at various levels.
